title: Setting due dates and returning grades in Assignments
what_it_is: A due date is the deadline set on a Teams Assignment, which can also trigger reminders to learners as it approaches. Returning grades is the step where a marked Assignment, with its grade and any feedback, is sent back to the learner.
why_use_it: A clear due date, backed up by an automatic reminder, means fewer missed deadlines without you having to chase every learner individually — and returning grades properly means a learner gets their result and feedback in one place, as soon as marking is done.
how_to: While creating or editing an Assignment in Teams, find the Due section and select the date and time you want work to be submitted by.
Teams automatically shows learners a countdown to the due date on their Assignments tab, and flags the Assignment if they haven't submitted as the deadline approaches.
To set an additional reminder, use the Schedule option to have the Assignment post to the channel at a chosen date and time, prompting learners before the deadline.
Once you've finished marking a submission, open it from the Assignment's grading view and check the grade shown is correct.
Select Return on that submission to send the grade and feedback back to the individual learner.
To return grades for everyone at once, select Return all from the top of the grading view — only do this once every submission you want to release has been marked.
